title: Classic pecan pie
subtitle: Sticky, dark pecan filling on a shortcrust base
excerpt: The American Southern classic: a shortcrust pastry shell filled with a sticky, dark pecan custard of golden syrup, brown sugar, butter, eggs and toasted pecans. One 23 cm pie, serving eight.
sourceNotes: Karo Cookbook (1908, public domain US trade pamphlet): documents the canonical pecan pie filling — golden syrup (or American 'corn syrup'), eggs, butter, sugar, pecans. Sarah Hutson and Mrs. Lincoln, Mrs. Lincoln's Boston Cook Book (1884), Project Gutenberg: documents shortcrust pastry blind-baking for syrup-based fillings.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the baked pie whole, wrapped twice; defrost overnight in the fridge.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Blind-bake the pastry up to a day ahead.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf — drops to 150°C after the first 20 minutes

recipeTools.notes: 23 cm loose-bottomed, or a pie dish.

content.text: Pecan pie has two parts: a blind-baked shortcrust shell and a sticky filling of golden syrup, brown sugar, melted butter, eggs and toasted pecans, poured in and baked until the centre just sets. The pecans float on top during the bake; the filling sets dark and glossy underneath.
Toast the pecans before they go in; the heat draws out the oils and deepens the flavour. The classic recipe uses American corn syrup, which has the same role as British golden syrup. Either works.

items.prepNote: yolk for the pastry, white reserved
halves; toast in a dry pan
200 ml — use corn syrup if you have it

content.text: Rub the {{unsalted-butter}} into the {{plain-flour}}, {{caster-sugar}} and first {{sea-salt-fine}}. Bind with the yolk of the {{eggs}} and the {{water}}. Wrap and chill an hour. Heat the oven to 180°C fan. Roll, line a 23 cm tin, trim and prick. Blind-bake with baking beans for 15 minutes; remove paper and beans and bake another 5 minutes. Brush warm pastry with the reserved egg white to seal.
Toast the pecans, mix the filling

Spread the {{pecans}} on a tray and toast in the oven for 5 minutes until they smell nutty. Cool. In a bowl, whisk the {{golden-syrup}}, {{soft-brown-sugar}}, melted {{unsalted-butter}}, beaten {{eggs}}, {{vanilla-extract}} and second {{sea-salt-fine}} to a smooth thin filling.

Drop the oven to 170°C fan. Scatter the toasted pecans into the pastry case and pour the filling over them. The pecans will float. Bake for 20 minutes, then drop to 150°C and bake for another 20 minutes; the edges should be set, the centre should wobble just slightly. Cool fully in the tin — the filling sets firm as it cools and slices cleanly once the pie is cold.
